design_philosophy: Modern minimal with a subtle Indian touch. High accessibility for non-technical family members. Generous touch targets, high-contrast text, clear explicit labeling over abstract icons.

rules: Headings should use Playfair Display to inject a premium, traditional wedding feel without heavy decor.
Body text must use Outfit for maximum legibility for older family members.
Never use font weights below 400 for text smaller than 18px.
Buttons and inputs must use body font, not heading font.
Generous Spacing: Leave ample breathing room. Use margin/padding 1.5x larger than standard apps.
Avoid complex grids. Use simple, stack-based layouts (vertical scroll) which are easier for non-technical users to navigate.

buttons.primary: bg-[#7A1A2A] text-white rounded-full h-14 flex items-center justify-center font-medium text-lg shadow-sm
buttons.secondary: bg-[#F3EFE6] text-[#7A1A2A] rounded-full h-14 flex items-center justify-center font-medium text-lg border border-[#E5E0D8]
rules: Minimum tap target height must be 48px, ideally 56px (h-14).
Use explicit text labels (e.g., 'Add New Guest') instead of just a '+' icon.
Add data-testid attributes to all interactive elements.

cards.base: bg-white rounded-2xl p-5 border border-[#E5E0D8] shadow-sm
rules: Cards should not have aggressive drop shadows. Keep it flat and clean.
Use muted gold (#C19B5E) for subtle highlights on cards, like a left border or small icon background.
Include text labels under all icons in the bottom tab bar.
Keep the tab bar background Ivory (#FCFAEF) with a subtle top border.

inputs.base: bg-white border border-[#E5E0D8] rounded-xl px-4 py-3 text-base text-[#1A1A1A] h-14
rules: Always show visible labels above inputs. Do not rely solely on placeholders.

accessibility.contrast: Ensure all text against the off-white background passes WCAG AA (minimum 4.5:1).
accessibility.touch_targets: All tappable areas must be at least 48x48 points.
accessibility.visual_hierarchy: Use bold font weights and larger sizes for primary information (e.g., Task Name). Use color strictly as a secondary indicator, not the only indicator of state.
accessibility.testing: All interactive and key informational elements MUST include a data-testid attribute (e.g., data-testid='login-button', data-testid='task-item-1').

image_urls.description: Subtle Indian wedding motifs, off-white background with maroon and gold line art for the Login Screen.
3D icon of a beautiful wedding diary/task list for the 'My Tasks' empty state.
3D icon of an elegant envelope/guest list for the 'Guests' empty state.

instructions_to_main_agent: This is a React Native Expo application. Adapt standard Tailwind CSS principles to NativeWind or standard StyleSheet.
Ensure all components are built using .js files (not .tsx).
The default screen after successful login MUST be 'My Tasks'.
Apply the deep maroon (#7A1A2A) as the primary action color (buttons, active tabs) and muted gold (#C19B5E) for secondary accents and warnings.
Keep navigation extremely simple. Avoid deep nesting. A bottom tab bar with Dashboard, Tasks, Guests, Budget, and Itinerary is ideal.
Do not use generic placeholder images. Use the provided image URLs for the login background and empty states.
For user roles (Admin/Contributor/Viewer), ensure UI conditionally renders elements (e.g., hide 'Add Guest' button for Viewers).
Prioritize large text and large buttons. Assume the user is a non-technical family member in their 50s/60s.
Every touchable element and key view MUST have a data-testid attribute in kebab-case.
